_note: Batch c (0-800 AD married-in spouses): documented parents + full siblings for spouses that had none shown. FILLED 4 of 6. SKIPPED 2 (St Helena, Basina of Thuringia) — their own parentage is undocumented/legendary. Web-verified 2026-07-16. New nodes use SPD_ prefix. Clotilde's mother Caretena is later tradition (Gregory of Tours does not name her) and is flagged as such.

a.label:
Publius Septimius Geta
c.110 AD · equestrian of Leptis Magna; father of the emperor · Wikipedia

b.label:
Fulvia Pia
c.125 AD · mother of Septimius Severus; of Leptis Magna · Wikipedia

children.label:
Publius Septimius Geta
d.c.204 AD · brother of the emperor; suffect & later ordinary consul · Wikipedia

Septimia Octavilla
c.150 AD · sister of Septimius Severus · Wikipedia

families.note: Publius Septimius Geta & Fulvia Pia -> Septimius Severus (+ brother P. Septimius Geta, sister Septimia Octavilla). Wikipedia / Cassius Dio.

a.label:
👑 Maximian
c.250-310 AD · Roman Emperor, Augustus of the West · Wikipedia

b.label:
Eutropia
c.255 AD · wife of Maximian; of Syrian origin · Wikipedia

children.label:
👑 Maxentius
c.283-312 AD · Roman Emperor; brother of Fausta; drowned at the Milvian Bridge · Wikipedia

families.note: Maximian & Eutropia -> Fausta + brother Maxentius. (Empress Theodora omitted — probably Eutropia's daughter by a prior marriage / Maximian's stepdaughter, disputed.) Wikipedia.

a.label:
👑 Chilperic II of Burgundy
c.450 AD · King of the Burgundians; father of Clotilde · Gregory of Tours

b.label:
Caretena
c.455 AD · TRADITIONALLY named as Clotilde's mother — Gregory of Tours does not name her · later tradition

children.label:
😇 Sedeleuba (Chrona)
c.470 AD · sister of Clotilde; became a nun, founded the church of St-Victor at Geneva · Gregory of Tours

families.note: Chilperic II of Burgundy (+ wife, traditionally Caretena) -> Clotilde + sister Sedeleuba/Chrona. Father & sister documented by Gregory of Tours; mother's name is later tradition (flagged).

a.label:
Gerold of Vinzgau
c.730-784 AD · Frankish Count of the Anglachgau; father of Hildegard · Wikipedia

b.label:
Emma (Imma) of Alemannia
c.735-798 AD · daughter of Hnabi, Duke of Alemannia; mother of Hildegard · Wikipedia

children.label:
Gerold the Younger
c.755-799 AD · Prefect of Bavaria; brother of Hildegard; killed fighting the Avars · Wikipedia

Udalrich
c.760 AD · Count of the Argengau & Linzgau; brother of Hildegard · Wikipedia

families.note: Gerold of Vinzgau & Emma of Alemannia (dau. of Duke Hnabi) -> Hildegard + brothers Gerold the Younger (Prefect of Bavaria) & Udalrich. Wikipedia.
